data.title: As an AI coding agent committing code, I receive session kudos recorded in a tamper-resistant ledger with anti-gaming controls and rank display
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Core kudos ledger infrastructure — the append-only JSONL ledger, award formula, deduplication, session ceiling, write guard hook, commit-validate.sh integration, session UUID, and rank display.
**Why**: This is the walking skeleton: without the ledger and formula, no trigger story can produce awards. All trigger stories (C, D, E, F) depend on this infrastructure existing.
**Scope**:
- IN: .kudos/ledger.jsonl schema, award formula, dedup, session ceiling, kudos-write-guard.sh PreToolUse hook, commit-validate.sh step in COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md, session UUID, rank display, dso-config.conf key, .gitignore pre-seeding, allowlist entry
- OUT: Individual trigger implementations (Stories C, D, E, F)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, .kudos/ledger.jsonl is created with all required fields: session_id, trigger_id, diff_hash, artifact_sha, verification_method, workflow_phase, message, points int>=1, jitter_factor float in [0.8,1.2], timestamp ISO-8601 UTC; all string fields JSON-escaped
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-1
- When this story is complete, Award(n) = round(BasePoints x (1/(n+1)) x jitter) is computed correctly; jitter in [0.8,1.2] uniform random; minimum 1 point; floating-point division; n counts prior entries for trigger_id within the CURRENT session only (session-scoped, not cumulative across sessions; .kudos/ is ephemeral/gitignored)
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-2
- When this story is complete, (trigger_id, diff_hash) deduplication is enforced per session: identical pairs produce no second award
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-3
- When this story is complete, kudos.session_ceiling=100 is in dso-config.conf with comment explaining the key; awards are blocked with a prohibition-block message when the ceiling is reached
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-4
- When this story is complete, session ceiling check and ledger.jsonl writes use flock for atomicity; concurrent agent commits in shared-directory mode cannot corrupt the file; TOCTOU race on ceiling check is prevented
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-4 concurrency safety
- When this story is complete, hook_kudos_write_guard function is registered in plugins/dso/hooks/lib/pre-edit-write-functions.sh and added to both pre-edit.sh and pre-write.sh dispatcher chains; blocks Edit/Write to .kudos/** with exit 2; jq-free parse_json_field pattern used
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-5
- When this story is complete, commit-validate.sh runs as a step in COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md (NOT as a git pre-commit hook); invoked with timeout 600000ms; each trigger writes to ledger independently (partial timeout is safe); per-worktree-review-commit.md compatibility verified; merge-to-main.sh ticket-sync commits excluded from kudos; checkpoint auto-save commits excluded
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-6
- When this story is complete, .kudos/ is pre-seeded in .gitignore in Story A's development commit (NOT added at runtime by commit-validate.sh — runtime modification creates chicken-and-egg with review gate)
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-6 .gitignore
- When this story is complete, .kudos/** is added to plugins/dso/hooks/lib/review-gate-allowlist.conf as defense in depth against accidentally-staged .kudos/ files blocking commits
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-6 defense in depth
- When this story is complete, session UUID is written to .kudos/session-id by commit-validate.sh on first run; fail-open: generates fresh UUID if .kudos/session-id is absent
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-7
- When this story is complete, contributor rank is displayed per commit: Contributor 0-25, Consistent 26-75, Distinguished 76-150, Exemplary 151-300, Principal Contributor 301+
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-8
- When this story is complete, an integration test demonstrates >= 3 triggers firing on a single synthetic commit; verifies deduplication, ceiling enforcement, and prohibition message text
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-4 cross-trigger ceiling
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] diff_hash computation: during implementation specify whether commit-validate.sh uses compute-diff-hash.sh (existing shared mechanism with per-commit caching) or independent computation; if independent, document why values may differ from review/test gate hashes.
- [Reliability] commit-validate.sh runs as COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md step (not pre-commit hook) to avoid the existing 8-hook chain's timeout constraints.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
